notes: Lesson 20 Orientation (act 116), 'Trie / Prefix Tree', 840.1s, 7 long cues. Running word set {car,card,cart,cat}. Uses trie.py idiom (TrieModel n-ary layout, TrieMobject char-labeled edges + amber end-of-word rings). Two review iterations: authored + manim -s smoke every cue (fixed layout: Cue00 chip-row scaled, Cue01 note below leaves, Cue02 edge-note relocated+legend clears d-node, Cue04 tree shrunk+card chip repositioned+all text guarded), then full -ql render + READ start/mid/end all 7 cues. Iteration 2 fixed a real Manim ring-hiding bug in Cue01 (wrapping live edge mobjects in a new VGroup for the SurroundingRectangle reparents them and hides the r/car end-ring) by building the spine box from node copies; that changed timing so combined the incremental edge+node reveals into single plays to keep cue01 on its 124.7s budget (partial-movie concat frames scale with animation count). Per-cue checklist (char-label legibility, trie-structure correctness incl shared prefix + end-of-word flags, highlighted element matches the beat, contrast, nothing off-frame via theme.guard, chunk timing within pinning tolerance): all pass. Final -ql 840.002s vs audio 840.07s (Δ0.068).
